sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, presents with chronic back pain that started 2 years ago, which he associates with moving furniture one weekend.
He describes the pain as sharp stabbing in his back, radiating down to his right leg and even to his right big toe.
He also experiences numbness and tingling in his right leg, particularly in the foot.
He has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past but with temporary relief.
He has been advised about surgery but wants to avoid it.
Past medical history: The patient quit smoking 20 years ago and does not recall any family members with spine conditions.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in the genital or rectal area, loss of sensation, weakness, or loss of control of bladder or bowels.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
Lumbar flexion causes pain.
Supine straight leg test is positive.
Decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; chronic lumbar back pain.
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation causing the pain.
The corticosteroid will be injected into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
I will monitor the patient and see how he responds to the injection.
The patient will be scheduled for up to 4 injections per year as needed.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for a follow-up appointment to monitor his response to the injection.
